analysis.semantic_summary: Parser for Imperva Sonar security logs that processes timestamp data and standardizes it into OCSF format. Primary focus is on temporal normalization of security events from Sonar's monitoring system.
optimization_opportunities: Add additional field mappings for richer context
Include error handling for malformed timestamps
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: imperva_sonar-latest
-- OCSF Class: Security Event (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:00:50.327009

-- Pre-declare locals for performance optimization
local type = type
local tonumber = tonumber
local os_time = os.time
local string_match = string.match
local ISO8601_PATTERN = "(%d+)%-(%d+)%-(%d+)T(%d+):(%d+):(%d+)%.?(%d*)"

-- Utility functions
local function parse_iso8601(timestamp_str)
    if not timestamp_str then return nil end
    
    local year, month, day, hour, min, sec, ms = string_match(timestamp_str, ISO8601_PATTERN)
    if not year then return nil end
    
    -- Convert to epoch seconds
    local time = os_time({
        year = tonumber(year),
        month = tonumber(month),
        day = tonumber(day),
        hour = tonumber(hour),
        min = tonumber(min),
        sec = tonumber(sec)
    })
    
    return time
end

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output structure with local reference
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "Security Event",
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "Security Control",
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Sonar",
                vendor_name = "Imperva"
            },
            version = "1.0.0"
        }
    }

    -- Extract and validate timestamp
    local timestamp = record.unmapped and record.unmapped.timestamp
    if timestamp then
        -- Try parsing as ISO8601 first
        local epoch_time = parse_iso8601(timestamp)
        if epoch_time then
            output.time = epoch_time
        else
            -- Fallback to direct numeric conversion
            output.time = tonumber(timestamp)
        end
    end

    -- Set default time if not present or invalid
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os_time()
    end

    -- Validate required fields
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    -- Additional field validations
    if type(output.metadata) ~= "table" then
        output.metadata = {}
    end

    -- Return transformed record
    return output
end

-- Error handling wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

test_cases.expected: Valid OCSF output with parsed timestamp
